claimText: Bharat Gen is India's first indigenously developed AI-based Multimodal Large Language Model (LLM) designed to support all 22 scheduled Indian languages.
evidenceExcerpt: Bharat Gen is India's first indigenously developed AI-based Multimodal LLM for all 22 scheduled Indian languages.
examUse: Statement-based question on the features and language support of Bharat Gen.
distractorHooks: Developed by a foreign tech giant
Is a text-only model, not multimodal

claimText: Bharat Gen is developed under the National Mission on Interdisciplinary Cyber-Physical Systems (NM-ICPS), implemented by IIT Bombay, and funded by the Department of Science and Technology (DST).
evidenceExcerpt: Bharat Gen is developed under NM-ICPS and implemented through TIH at IIT Bombay with DST funding.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the implementing institution and funding ministry for Bharat Gen.
distractorHooks: Funded by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)

claimText: The IndiaAI Mission is implemented by the IndiaAI Independent Business Division (IBD) under the Digital India Corporation of MeitY.
evidenceExcerpt: IndiaAI Mission is implemented by the IndiaAI IBD under Digital India Corporation (MeitY).
examUse: Statement-based question on the nodal ministry and implementing body of the IndiaAI Mission.
distractorHooks: Under the Ministry of Science and Technology
Implemented directly by the Prime Minister's Office

claimText: AIRAWAT is a common compute platform dedicated to facilitating AI research.
evidenceExcerpt: AIRAWAT provides a common compute platform for AI research.
examUse: Match-the-following or correct-pair question identifying the purpose of AIRAWAT.
distractorHooks: AIRAWAT is an indigenous AI chatbot
AIRAWAT is a military drone system
AIRAWAT is a quantum computing framework

claimText: The Bletchley Declaration of 2023 is an international agreement signed by 28 countries and the EU to establish a shared understanding of the risks associated with frontier AI.
evidenceExcerpt: Bletchley Declaration (2023) establishes a shared understanding of frontier AI risks, signed by 28 countries and the EU.
examUse: Statement-based question on international AI governance initiatives and the specific focus of the Bletchley Declaration.
distractorHooks: Bletchley Declaration focuses on cryptocurrency regulation
Aims to completely ban the development of frontier AI
